entry.title: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on RISE & SAP BTP

    🧰👷‍Toolbox for SAP Technology Architects👷‍🧰

 

 

RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE) is re-writing the book on how companies "do" SAP, and infact, everybody on that journey or planning for or preparing for that journey is re-writing the book.

There are so many myths and misunderstandings about RISE, hopefully this work will shine more light on the subject.

The goal of this work is to provide a SAP RISE "1 Pager" for all of us Customer/System Integrator side folks who are on this journey. 

And back in 2013 when I was studying for SAP HANA Certification I published the following blog, as a central point of reference for SAP HANA technical information, it helped me with what I was studying and helped others with having piles of very useful information in one place:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/the-sap-hana-reference-for-sap-basis-administrators/ba-p/13007539

Fast forward 12 years, and we are now in the same situation with SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP, there is useful documentation all over the place and it would help if there was a central location for SAP Technology Architects to use as a "Toolbox". It will certainly help me 🙂

Implementing and Migrating to and Running S/4 PCE on RISE for the last two years, and as usual collecting a mountain of the most useful documentation as a personal toolbox, it's been clear to see that similar to the situation back in the earlier period of HANA, there is useful RISE documentation in a number of locations, this blog will act as a central resource pointing to all of the locations of the SAP S/4 PCE RISE documentation.

And similarly for the BTP, having been doing BTP (or as it was the SAP Cloud Platform) for 7 years, this blog will be a central location pointing to the most useful BTP documentation for SAP Technology Architects.

The two subjects are combined, SAP S/4HANA PCE on RISE with BTP, because in my eyes the two go hand in hand, S/4HANA PCE on RISE is the next generation of the previous On-Premise ERP offerings from SAP, and the BTP contains the next generation of Applications which we will all use with the S/4HANA PCE on RISE, including for example, and not limited to, Integration Suite and CPI & EM/AEM & APIM, obviously CALM as this is replacing in many but not all cases what we do with SolMan, and all of the other Services which we will need pretty much day by day to run S/4HANA PCE on RISE, and of course, Steampunk, keeping the Core Clean, and "Upgrade Stable" (as my friend Sakthivel Sivaguru would say), then we need the ABAP Service in the BTP for the side car extensions. But that's just the BTP "Starter Pack", as you go on the BTP journey you will be using all kinds of Services and including the AI Joule and the Business Application Studio and the Work Zone, Custom Domain, ADS, Secure Logon, CTMS  etc and then of course the Intelligent Services across the Business Processes, eg Intelligent Account Reconciliation, there's a link to an Excel sheet here, which lists all of the Business Processes and how they are Intelligently enabled by SAP Technologies.

If you have links to useful documentation which you think will help others and should be added to the list, then please just write it in the comments and I will add it to the list.

For 25 years I have been hunting and collecting SAP documentation, my library spans the whole journey from before the New Dimension Products, through NetWeaver 2004, through HANA DB, through S/4HANA, through SAP Cloud Platform and on through the current phase S/4HANA Cloud aka "Grow with SAP", S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition aka "RISE with SAP", and the Business Technology Platform and the Intelligent Technologies. Hunting a collecting documentation is a useful skill, and here's a tip from my friend Jarod Sandham - some useful search criteria to find documentation is to search "https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/ <insert search criteria here>, eg open google and search for:  https://assets.dm.ux.sap.com/  security

Please expect to find changes and new information here, because it will be a live work in progress, it's going to take a few weeks to list and classify everything that I already have. Plans for immediate future are for each link to add a screenshot of the most useful information behind that link, and that's going to take a few weeks, and at the same time the list will just get longer.

And finally the ask to SAP... if friends from SAP are reading this work, and know about new versions of documents/materials or new documents/materials which are available either publicly or with an S User and could enrich this work, please let me know either directly or in the chat and I will incorporate that material to the benefit of everyone.

Disclaimer: For the record, regarding the pictures and screenshots, ALL pictures are from documents which are publicly available and searchable on google, there are no pictures from any SAP documentation which is protected behind an authentication mechanism. There are many links here to documents where you will need to have an S User to be able to read them, and there are no screenshots from any of those documents. Every screenshot/picture contains a statement that this document is publicly available on the Internet.

 

 

Ok, let's go 🚀

 

 

Contents:

Section 1.02] The S/4HANA 3 Transition & Migration & Implementation Options

Section 1.1] Implementing and Running S/4HANA PCE Private Cloud Edition on SAP RISE

Section 5.2] Extending S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 5.3] Integrating S/4HANA on RISE - UNDER PREPARATION

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

Section 7] Lessons Learned

_________________________________________________________________

 

Section 1] General Introduction Documents and RISE Getting Started Guides

What is RISE from the viewpoint of a SAP Technology Architect ?  

The following picture is from the publicly available document Presentation Title

How do you do RISE ?

Regardless of what anybody thinks about all of the different methodologies, every SAP Customer who is contemplating the move to S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition on RISE must begin by reading about the "RISE with SAP Methodology".

This is basically a guided procedure including supporting documentation to help SAP Customers get to S/4HANA in the smoothest and straightest line possible. 

Naturally SAP have a recommended tool chain to support for following the SAP RISE Methodology, and regardless of whether on not you use SAP's recommended tools, this picture is very useful because it elaborates a little more what is happening at each part of the journey:

Here's an introduction to the methodology RISE with SAP | Methodology

This is the overview of the guided procedure:

This picture comes from this document which is publicly available: Mapping your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition : A practical guide for senior IT leadership

These are the core tasks of the methodology:

I recommend everybody who will be part of the journey to read that document as one of the first steps, once everybody has read that document, everybody will be on the same page as to what lies ahead.

Here are more documents which are very useful during the preparation:

Section 1.01] The SAP & Customer Shared Responsibilities on RISE

This section is exceptionally important, from the SAP Basis and Operations perspective there are naturally a lot of questions around, how does the Basis function work on RISE. What does SAP do, what does the Customer (and their SI) do ? There are a lot of misunderstandings and incorrect myths.

The goal of this section is to help the SAP Customer who is thinking about RISE to build a cleaer picture and understanding of what the responsibilities are and what the segregation of duties are.

A lot of people seem to think that on RISE the Customer won't need a Basis Team, well, far from it, on RISE the Customer obviously won't need so many Basis people, but, and the big but is, the Customer will need exceptionally qualified and experienced Basis people who at the same time have a very good maturity and discipline in their behaviour. One of my biggest lessons, and there are many many learnings, has been, with RISE, due to the nature of the shared responsibilities, there is very little room for trial and error in the Basis Domain, and the Basis Team need to put massive effort in to proper planning and preparation of activities and understand who will do what bases upon the segregation of duties between SAP and the Customer. There is no room for trial and error when doing new things in the Basis area.

So, we could write a book about that, but we are here to share useful documentation, so here it comes...

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition Security and Compliance

Don't be deceived by the title, this document contains some golden information, anybody wanting to understand who does what with RISE, here are pictures you need, these picture show where your responsibilities are:

We start with a higher level view which is from the RISE OnBoarding Handbook Onboarding Handbook .pdf

In my opinion the above picture is not detailed enough, it doesn't communicated really how much responsibility the Customer SAP Basis Team will have. (The detailed list of ECS Services is here: SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition - Service Specifications)

The following pictures go into some more detail on the Customer's SAP Basis responsibilities:

The above pictures are from the publicly available document Check & ProductivityPlus Training

RISE with SAP: Shared Security Responsibility for SAP Cloud Services

There are some great pictures and explanations in this blog, like this one:

 

Speaking from first hand experience this is how I see the responsibilities, this can of course all by done by SAP ECS by the Customer buying additional Services:

Customer / SI

Dedicated Private Connectivity to Hyperscaler
Configuration of Business Processes
Application Administration and Management
Application Identity Management
Application Authentication and Authorisation
Business Roles, User Groups, Access Control
Compliance Regulations
Application Security Audit Logs
API Extension, Integration and 3rd Party Connectivity
Application Change Management
Performance Management
Advanced Security & Compliance
Functional Application Management
Deployment Management
Testing
Release Management
Business Improvement
Data LifeCycle Management
Operations Improvement
Advanced Application / Solution Monitoring
Application Security Updates
Continuous Operations

SAP ECS

SW Subscription / Support
Reference Architecture
Technical System Operations
HA / DR
OS/ DB
Compute/Storage/Network
Threat Management and Patch Management
Operational Security

Section 1.02] The S/4HANA 3 Transition & Migration & Implementation Options

There are three ways to get to S/4HANA PCE on RISE:

Greenfield - new clean S/4HANA

Bluefield Selective Data Transition - search this page for Bluefield and you will find the latest guides on how to do this

Brownfield Transformation - transform and convert an existing ECC in to an S/4HANA

This document is publicly available: 20230328_sap_dmlt.pdf

I'm not going to write much more on this in my own words because there is so much very useful documentation available from SAP on this subject.

This section of the Toolbox will provide links to lots of useful supporting documents and highlight very useful diagrams which will help for understanding your situation and your options.

It's less about tossing a coin and more about understanding what you want to achieve and where you want to get to. The following documents and pictures will explain why...

Please note some of these documents are older and some are newer, the older ones are using older terminology, but the essentials are the same and that's why they are included here, because the message they communicate is useful

These pictures are from this publicly available document: Microsoft PowerPoint - S4HANA RIG @ User Groups.pptx

Section 2] SAP ECS Enterprise Cloud Services

There is not too much information in the public domain on what the SAP ECS Team is doing, they are your Partner for pretty much everything, they are like your Cloud/Unix/Technical-Layer-Basis wrapped in to one, the majority of the really useful documentation on what the ECS Team do and how to work with them is listed below, but, you will need an S User and in some cases need to be a RISE Customer to access the documentation.

This section of the Toolbox is really aimed at Basis people and Unix Team / Cloud Hosting / Network people from the Customer side, and so, I am including here the most useful pictures from documents in the public domain to help with introductions as to how the networking to SAP ECS is working, how the hosting is working and how the security of it all is working.

The following picture is from the publicly available document Presentation Title

The picture helps us all to understand what the SAP ECS Team are doing, what parts of the stack they are responsible for:

This is a nice overview of SAP Cloud Application Services for RISE Customers, this document is publicly available: Presentation Title

 

 

And now some useful pictures on how the networking and HA and DR are working, these pictures are from the publicly available document  220818_rise_with_sap_sap_hana_cloud_private_edtition.pdf

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

And then here we have some great insights to the HA and DR setup... there are other documents, they are listed in this 1 Pager and you will need an S User for those, just search this list for HA and DR...

Section 5] SAP BTP General

SAP Business Technology Platform BTP is a massive Platform, and in my opinion will be bigger and more important than the S/4HANA for SAP Customers in the future. The BTP is a technology enabler containing all kinds of capabilities.

The first thing I am going to comment on, since this is a 1 Pager for SAP Technology Architects is the Technical Architecture of the BTP.

The BTP is extremely rich and flexible in how it can be set up and configured, and this can be a good thing and a bad thing, the biggest challenge is, making foundation layer mistakes which cost time and money to correct in the future.

For me to try show how complex the BTP is, we will first look at SAP's Future Ready Enterprise Framework, this is an approach to modularising maturity with the BTP and leading the Customer on a journey to BTP Architecture Excellence.

You've heard of a SAP Customer Center of Excellence, there is now a concept of SAP BTP Center of Excellence, this shows how complex and rich this subject is.

There is a beautiful publicly available presentation here  on the Future Ready Enterprise and the SAP BTP Future Ready Enterprise Unlock and Maximize the value of BTP

With wonderful slides like these...

 

 

 

You don't necessarily need to engage SAP's Services on this, at least by studying and digesting the picture you can think about how those dimensions apply to your Organisation and fill in the boxes in terms of the objectives and vision of your Organisation

 

 

 

These slides are a humbling reminder of how vast the BTP is, and how getting a decent maturity level in the BTP will help your Organisation achieve its goals

 

 

So now we know, the BTP is big, and it's complex, and it needs proper planning and architecting if we are going to get the most from it.

For  the first few years the SAP Cloud Platform was mainly SAP Cloud Platform Integration and a lot of people slipped in to a bad habit of associating the BTP with Integration capability, we it's a lot more than that and we all need to appreciate that.

The following document is another useful introduction to the SAP Future Ready Enterprise Framework:

Presentation Title

The following picture is here to show us just how complex the BTP is, and let us all reflect on where our Company's maturity level is with the BTP:

 

 

Just spend some time to study that table and think about where your Organisation's maturity level is and whether or not it would be a good investment to buy some BTP Training and Services from SAP. I am not trying to sell Services here, but I have learned the hard way that an investment in knowledge can save significant amounts in the future.

This picture which is from the same deck is to really show us what a journey BTP maturity is...

 

There is a very nice blog by @Pedro_Souza  on the same subject here The Future-Ready Enterprise: A Framework for SAP B... - SAP Community

The next step for the SAP Technology Architect is to read the BTP Admin Guide SAP BTP Administrator's Guide | SAP Help Portal  and read the section on SetUp and Plan Set Up and Plan | SAP Help Portal the whole section including the Governance Model. 

Then read the section Setting up your Account Model Setting Up Your Account Model | SAP Help Portal

The biggest lesson I can bring is, when architecting the BTP from a clean sheet of paper, one of the most important foundation steps is to correctly design the Stages / Directories / Sub-Accounts and Entitlements, the setting up of the Account Model, get this wrong and you will pay for it later.

BTP Domains / Stages / Directories / Sub-Accounts

Domain refers to the Capability, eg Cloud Integration, Work Zone, Build Process Automation

Stages refers to the type of environment, eg Dev, Test, Quality, UAT, Prod

Directory refers to the structuring elements for Sub-Accounts

Sub-Account let's you further structure according to your requirements

My recomendation is go for an Account Model with Directories and Sub-Accounts Account Model With Directories and Subaccounts | SAP Help Portal

The reason for this is:

You then have the possibility for all kinds of template BTP layouts according to the demands of your Organisation and at the same time you can leverage all of the benefits described above

 

 

 

And then as already described you profit from being able to leverage Authorisation and Entitlement features per Directory / BTP Domain

Setting Up Your Account Model | SAP Help Portal

To recap setting up the Account Model is probably the most important BTP Technical Architecture decision !

This is the Future Ready Enterprise document which is referred to in the RISE Onboarding Handbook FRE Best Practice Guide for RISE Transformation_Private Cloud.pdf

Next step go to the SAP Discovery Center and look at the BTP OnBoarding material SAP Discovery Center Mission Public Card - Live and OnDemand Webinars about SAP BTP Onboarding

 

And then go to the SAP BTP OnBoarding center SAP Business Technology Platform General Concepts

For SAP Build and all of the Services around it have a look at this presentation

2023_sap_build_webinar_april_4.pdf

 

For the BTP another very good place to start is here: Future Ready Best Practice Guide for RISE Transformation_Private Cloud.pdf

The document contains very nice descriptions of the most important BTP Services and also Reference Architecture pictures recommending which BTP Services to have and where and why depending upon the type of move to S/4HANA on RISE that you are doing

 

 

 

and very nice BTP Services descriptions like this one for Security and Identity Management in the BTP

 

 

and then you can move on the following documents...

Section 5.2] SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration CI

BTP CI IFlows with AI Assistance

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/creating-integration-flow#generate-integrations-with-ai-assistance

2903776 - SAP Integration Suite – Service Plans and Upgrade Paths

https://me.sap.com/notes/2903776

SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology

https://help.sap.com/doc/ac5a3b73452548cb887f3963877eb9ab/Cloud/en-US/sap.integration.solutions.advisory.pdf

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration

 

Section 5.3] AI & Intelligent Technologies

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-generative-ai.html

Intelligent Technologies and Business Processes Excel Sheet

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/hHzXdEW?rc=10

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3393699

 

 

Section 6] SAP Customer Center of Excellence

(This section is a work in progress)

You might be asking yourself why in a SAP RISE Toolbox there are links to how to do a SAP CCOE ?

That's a great question, and the answer is that, most companies have been running/operating SAP the same way since 3.1h, and now with RISE the whole game is changing, the Roles and Responsibilities are changing and the Segregation of Duties is changing, between who does what, SAP, Customer, and if they have one the Customer's System Integrator. 

And that's why, during the S/4HANA RISE Transformation, you will be best off, if you take the opportunity and redesign how you run/operate SAP.

The SAP Customer CoE Journey:

Where are you today ? And where do you want to be ?

The above pictures are publicly available and come from this document: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

Guides and Best Practices for Customer COE

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

SAP Customer CoE Strategy, Governance, and Organisation

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

Landscape Management Process

https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/landscape-management-process.html?anchorId=section_175842419

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/business-transformation-requires-organizational-transformation/ba-p/13511386

CCOE Media Library

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/media-library.html

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discover-organizational-change-management-for-sap-cloud-projects/how-is-sap-s-organizational-change-management-framework-structured_c1708e6a-28f8-4245-8392-d83cbf48773b

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discover-organizational-change-management-for-sap-cloud-projects

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html?anchorId=section

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe-hints.pdf

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe.html?anchorId=section_541877121_co

https://support.sap.com/en/offerings-programs/ccoe/cio-customer-coe-guides.html

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/applying-the-sap-enterprise-architecture-framework

This is really useful

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-as-service-provider/primary-ccoe/CCOE_GettingStarted.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_strategy_framework.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_licenses_framework.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_information_knowledge.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_supportability_en.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/ccoe-document-store/ccoe_continuous_success_en.pdf

https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/customer-center-of-expertise/media-library/general-information/continuous-improvement-and-innovation-with-a-ccoe.pdf

 

 

Section 7] Lessons Learned

(this section is an ongoing work in progress)

Don't skip "Phase 0", plan to give Phase Zero the time it needs

Key Users

Key Users are one of the keys to a successful GoLive make sure they are trained and have all of the support they need

 
Key Users are your Ambassadors, make sure they have everything they need
 
Investment and Multi-Year Multiple Release/GoLive Transformations
 
Everything is relative, whatever you can afford, make sure to invest in the success of the first GoLive/Release,do not cut costs or corners on the first GoLive/Release, the first GoLive/Release MUST be a SUCCESS.
 
Subsequent GoLives/Releases will in general be a rinse and repeat of the template for how
the first GoLive/Release was executed and you can reduce costs on those GoLives/Releases
 
S/4 Data Transformation and Integration
 
We need to do Mapping and Transformations of Data, either at the SAP End or the other End - this is another challenge to the "Clean-Core" and the S/4 Transformations, there needs to be a principle, mappings/transformation must happen outside of the S/4
 
Technical GoLive should be separated / disconnected from the Business GoLive
 
Technical GoLive several months before Business GoLive

RISE Proactive Maintenance for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Contractual Maintenance Periods

SAP and Customer agree regular periods where there can be downtimes for SAP to do Maintenance

Plan this in your Release Planning

S/4HANA Upgrades

In your multi-year Release Plan, make room for S/4HANA Upgrades to enable you to benefit from the latest SAP innovations

Capacity

You need to care for your Capacity and Data Volume Management

Basis Projects and Operations

You need to re-design your Project Basis Team and Operations Basis Team and methods of working according to the segregation of duties with the RISE ECS Team

Run SGEN straight after systems are delivered and before GoLives

Performance Tuning & House Keeping  - that's your job

FUE Licensing Costs and SAP Roles and Authorisations

SAP RISE S/4HANA usage is measured and sized in what’s called FUE’s, Full Usage Equivalents​, pay attention to this

SAP RISE AntiVirus

VSI is a extra service, not mandatory as of today

EWA Report

Pay very close to attention to it, it will become one of your best friends

S/4HANA FailOver and DR

Understand what you really get with 99.7 and 99.9 regarding Availability Zones and Regions

S/4HANA Preparation

Before installation, specify all Security Requirements and get them included in the installation and post installation

RISE is about Segregation of Duties on the Basis Team

RISE is about running Basis as a Factory with clear Roles and Responsibilities and Methods or Working

RISE is about Proper Planning an Preparation

Basis Trial and Error way of working evolves to Planning and Preparation way of working

 
Service Requests
 
Quality
 
Right First Time
 
SAP do not quality control Service Requests
 
Learn the Templates
 
RISE Technical Architecture
 
Is not the same as OnPremise
 
A RISE LoadBalancer or Application Gateway, does not necessarily work the same way on RISE as it does in your own DataCenter/HyperScaler even if they are the same
 
The SAP RISE Architecture View
 
Get from SAP as much technical architecture views of the RISE "Customer Setup" as possible
 
SAP Technical Architects are the an integral part of the new Basis Team
 
Basis Team benefits from being experienced and disciplined 
 
RISE Architect from SAP
 
Get a SAP RISE Architect from SAP onboarded to the project from the beginning

SAP CALM is your new best friend

Sizing and Performance Testing better in than out

Trust and Verify

There are very useful RISE lessons learned here from @shaji_narayanan 

Lessons Learned from SAP ERP Conversion to RISE wi... - SAP Community

 

And most of all, enjoy the RISE Journey 🛫

 

Andy Silvey.

SAP Technology Architect [Contractor]

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

entry.title: My Home in SAP S/4HANA - A Deep Dive as of 2408
entry.content:
My Home is your personalized entry page within SAP S/4HANA,  that provides you with easy access to the business insights, tasks, and applications you need to do your job with greater speed and agility. My Home lets you access and manage your to-dos, your favorite pages, your favorite apps, and your insights on any device. It builds on the functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad and leverages Spaces and Pages (as a prerequisite). The new My Home was first released with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208 release. Since its first release, there have been great improvements in its functionality and user experience. In this blog, we’ll revisit the features of My Home that will enhance your user experience and productivity.  

Video: Welcome to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA 

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

 

My Home Sections 

 

The My Home entry page is divided into different sections. You can personalize the content of each section to fit your specific needs. 

 

To-Dos 

The To-Dos section provides an overview of your daily tasks. It combines your tasks from the My Inbox application and your situations from the standard and extended My Situations applications. The tasks display relevant business information about your assigned workflow items. It enables you to process your tasks without ever having to leave My Home. If Collaborative ERP is enabled on the system, you can collaborate directly with colleagues via MS Teams to resolve issues about your tasks quickly.  

Video: Managing the To-Dos Section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home To Do Section

 

 

 

News 

The News section keeps you updated on your company's latest news, or SAP S/4HANA system news. If SAP S/4HANA system news is displaying, you can shuffle through system news from different lines of business. You can manage SAP S/4HANA system news by choosing a line of business  that interests you to stay focused on relevant information. 

Video: Personalizing the News section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home News Section

 

 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home System News articles

 

 

 

Pages  

The Pages section provides you with quick access to your favorite or most used pages. You can manage the visibility of your pages and order them in a way that enhances your productivity.  

Video: Personalizing the Pages section 

SAP S/4HANA My Home Pages Section

 

 

 

Apps 

The Apps section provides easy access to your favorite, most used, recently used, and recommended applications. The Apps section improves the discoverability of your applications. The Recommended tab offers app suggestions using SAP Business AI, based on your user-specific role assignment. You can add apps to the Favorites tab from your most used, recently used, and recommended applications. You can organize and categorize your favorite applications by using the grouping, ordering and color-coding functionality so you can access them even more efficiently. 

Video: Personalizing the Apps section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Apps Section

 

 

 

Insights Tiles and Insights Cards 

The Insights section allows you to manage and monitor your company’s activities. It shows critical business information through KPIs and micro charts. This section is broken into two sections: Insights Tiles and Insights Cards. Insights cards and tiles display dynamic content at a glance. They provide quick and easy access to the applications that can provide more detailed information. Insights tiles offer analytical information, such as charts or KPIs. Insights cards are similar to Insights tiles but provide more detailed analytical information in the form of charts, tables or lists from supporting overview page-, list report-, and analytical list page-based applications. Thanks to this information, you can assess what is happening at any time and take action when needed.  You can customize the Insights section to display information that enables you to do your job with speed and agility. 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Tiles Section 

Video: Personalizing the Insights Cards Section 

 SAP S/4HANA My Home Insights Section

 

 

The My Home Layout and Advanced Settings 

 You can personalize the individual My Home sections, and another key feature is the ability to customize the layout of these sections on My Home. This personalization capability allows you to prioritize the information that is most important to you. You can manage the visibility and the display order of each of the My Home sections. 

Video: Personalizing the Layout of My Home  

Once you have personalized the My Home sections you can easily share your personalized content with co-workers who have the same or a similar role as you. This  enables them to also work with speed and agility. 

Video: Sharing the Personalized Content 

 

 

 

Now that we have given you a recap of the available My Home functionality in SAP S/4HANA, we hope you enjoy working with My Home in SAP S/4HANA and reap its benefits. You can  look forward to more enhancements that will improve your My Home experience even further in future releases of SAP S/4HANA.  

Note: Innovations appear first in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The demos in the videos were recorded on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in September 2024. 

Stay tuned! 

Caroline Welsh and Anna Freitag 

Product Managers of My Home, Cloud ERP UX Engineering.

entry.title: New fields in the EFD – Reinf Report (Brazil) Migration Object
entry.content:
Hello,

Hello SAP Community,

I am excited to share some important updates with you! The EFD – Reinf report (Brazil) migration object has recently been enhanced to include new fields that will assist you in migrating your data. Each of these new fields is associated with specific EFD-Reinf events, as listed below.

For the R-1050 event:

  • Classification of the Related Entity (TP_ENT_LIG)
  • CNPJ of the Related Entity (CNPJ_LIG)

For the R-4099 event:

  • Closing or Reopening Indicative (FECH_RET)

For the R-4080 event:

  • CNPJ of the Source Paying the Income (CNPJ_FONT)

For the R-4010 event:

  •  Beneficiary's CPF (CPF_BENEF)
  • Beneficiary's Name (NM_BENEF)
  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

For the R-4020 event:

  • Beneficiary's Name (NM_BENEF)
  • Beneficiary's CNPJ (CNPJ_BENEF)
  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

For the R-4040 event:

  • ID of Additional File Sent by Benefic. (IDE_ADIC_BENEF)

To learn more about these updates and stay informed with our latest news, I recommend you visit the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • About the migration object: EFD - Reinf report (Brazil)
  • EFD-Reinf Report product assistance page. 
  • Available Migration Objects

 

We would love your feedback. If you have any questions or suggestions for an upcoming post, please leave your comment below. 

See you next time!

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance 

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: Uploading External File in Adobe Forms
entry.content:
UPLOADING EXTERNAL FILE IN ADOBE FORMS 

Uploading External file : 

Scenario: 

A company needs to process vendor invoices in the SAP system, and as part of the Invoice Verification process, employees need to upload supporting documents (such as PDF invoices or scanned images) for audit and payment processing. These documents are often required to be attached to the invoice in SAP for review, verification, and audit purposes. 

In this scenario, Adobe Forms is used to facilitate the upload of the vendor's invoice document directly into the SAP system during the invoice verification process. 

Advantages of Uploading external file in adobe forms: 

  • Reduce Manual Errors 
  • Automatic Document Linking 
  • User-Friendly Interface 
  • Reduce paper usage 
  • Time Saving 

Disadvantages of uploading external file in adobe forms: 

  • Complexity in implementation 
  • System performance and Storage issues 
  • Limited File  type support 
  • Dependence on Adobe and SAP Versions 
  • Cost of Adobe License and Tools  

Here I'm going to upload a below Form. 

Note: File should be PDF Format.

Steps to Upload External file in Adobe forms: 

Step 1: Steps to create Interface: 

  • Goto SFP. 

  • Select the Interface radio button. 

  • Provide Interface name and click on create. 

  • Provide description , Interface type and click on save. 

  • Provide the fields based on your form. 

Step 2: Steps to create Form: 

  • Goto SFP. 

  • Select the Form radio button. 

  • Provide form name and click on create. 

  • Provide description and Interface name and click on save. 

  • Drag and Drop the fields into Adobe form area the fields which  is required for form. 

  • Click on layout for designing the form. 

  • Go to Tools in menu bar. 

  • Click Import node and select the file/template and click on open. 

  • Select the import options based on your requirement and click on Ok. 

  • The template/file is uploaded. 

  • Map the fields from your interface to your temple. 

  • Select the Data view tab in template, it will display the interface parameters. 

  • Mapping(Drag and drop) the parameters based on your field names in form. 

  • Place the parameters to the appropriate place. 
  • If you want to place/mention the same form field name or you want to apply any font styles/Formatting style you can select Object node under Palette menu. 

  • If you want to make any adjustments to fields with the help of Object you can provide. 

  • Adjust the importing/interface parameters based on form fields. 

  • Click on Save. 

  • Active the Form and click on Execute. 
  • Provide the input values and click on execute. 

  • Provide output device type as LPO1 and click on Print Preview.

entry.title: Must know tricks for GRC (Access Control / Process Control) or Security Consultants
entry.content:
In this article, I aim to simplify this process by providing insights into identifying SPRO node technical details. Based on my experience, I’ve observed that many users struggle to compile a complete list of the T-codes maintained under SPRO nodes. Before diving into the technical details, let’s first review some critical challenges in enterprise risk management and access control.

 

Key challenges in S/4HANA Role Design

  1. Excessive Access:
  1. Misunderstanding Reporting T-Codes:
    It is common for T-codes starting with S_ALR* or S_* to be misinterpreted as simple display reports. However, this assumption is incorrect, as many of these T-codes grant significant operational capabilities.
  2. SM30 (Table Maintenance View):
    This customizing object allows for the maintenance of values in authorization objects. If improperly restricted, it can introduce risks due to its range-based value maintenance capability.
  1. Role design issue with specific SPRO activity:

 

SPRO activities are commonly used to setup up the system and perform project specific customising settings. However, it’s often good practice to restrict the access per specific node.

 

An Opportunity to improve SAP GRC Access Control Ruleset:

  1. Critical Action and Permission Checks:
    While designing the ruleset, organisations often overlook key configurations maintained exclusively through SPRO and its relevant maintenance views. These configurations are linked to SPRO nodes that utilize specific T-codes, which may be inadvertently assigned to users through roles.

Solution:
To identify the relevant maintenance views linked to SPRO nodes, you can refer to the table CUS_IMGACH. This table provides direct access to details about SPRO nodes and their associated T-codes.

 

 

 

SOX and Financial Control Documentation

Documenting SOX or financial controls becomes significantly easier when you can identify the relevant T-codes calling maintenance views. These views provide insights into the underlying tables, enabling more efficient and accurate documentation. Below is a step-by-step approach:

Steps to Extract Relevant Maintenance Views:

  1. Download the SPRO Node List from CUS_IMGACH:
    • Export the list into an Excel file for further analysis.

 

  1. Extract the T-code List from Table USOBT_C:
    • Use a transaction like SE16 to download the relevant T-code data.

 

 

 

  1. Match Maintenance Views with VLOOKUP in Excel:
  2. Perform a VLOOKUP with sheet (1. Download the SPRO Node List from CUS_IMGACH) to correlate T-codes with their corresponding maintenance views.
    • Note: Maintenance views typically have a prefix “V_” added to the table name.

 

However, if you have still not able to find the details then copy the view name and refer the details below per below screenshots:

 

  1. Verify Details in SE11:
      • Select the "View" radio button. In this example I have chosen view “V_T052” which is mainly used to maintain details for “Terms of Payment”

 

      • Enter the maintenance view or table name associated with the SPRO node’s T-code.

 

 

 

On left hand side under tab Table/Join Conditions you may find the details of table and fields.

Feel free add your valuable inputs.

entry.title: New Installation of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 – Part 1 – Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
This procedure is Part 1 of the series which describes the very first steps to download the media. We will focus on creating Sandbox type instance with co-deployed (embedded) gateway.

Please find other blogs as follows at S/4HANA SQUARE ONE


                  SAP S/4HANA  SQUARE  ONE

GREENFIELD IMPLEMENTATION    PART1 - MPPART2 -  Installation
PART3 -  BP Activation
PART4 - Fiori ActivationPART5 - DATA MIGRATION
S/4HANA 2023fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2022fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2021fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2020fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps0

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

 


You need to login to SAP Maintenance Planner to access system assigned to your S-user. Please ensure landscape data is uploaded to SAP support portal via LMDB of SAP Solution Manager. The diagram below shows the Maintenance planner cloud service and how it integrates with Customer system.

 

 


The source system is first defined in Maintenance planner (1) generates (2) stack.xml, used for (3) Installation and (4) Upgrade and then system is (5) registered with Solution Manager. The system data is synchronized with Landscape Management Database (LMDB). From LMDB the data is (6) uploaded to customer profile in SAP support portal. (7) Data Fetch occurs using Solman daily job – “Landscape Fetch”.

If there is issue in replicating you may be asked to apply Note 2186164 – Problem in replicating systems to SAP Maintenance Planner

TIP: This data based on Customer Number is used by Maintenance Planner for all planning activities. Also ensure you use same Customer Number to define system in sapnet. Use the S-user id of this Customer Number.

Login to SAP Maintenance Planner and select the SAP Fiori tile Plan for SAP S/4HANA

In the next screen,choose Install a new SAP S/4HANA system, Manual Installation. Here you can click on the link for the SAP S/4HANA on SAP Help Portal for the documentation and click on Next.

In the next screen enter SID = S4H; select Target Version = SAP S/4HANA 2023; Target Stack = 02 (10/2024) FPS
TIP: The component SAP Predictive integrator is selected by default but please note this component will not be installed by SWPM/SUM but has to be manually installed on the database.
Click on Next.
TIP: The selection of component above will only download the files. You have to install them as per the procedure for the component. 

In the next screen choose Co-deployed with Backend. Please refer SAP Fiori Deployment Options and Landscape recommendations for detailed information. Please select checkmark Target Product Instances as applicable depending on which SAP FontEnd Applications you need among UI for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Human Capital Management, SAP Master Data Governance and SAP Travel Management.

In the next scree click on Continue Planning

In the next screen Click on Next

In the next screen select OS/DB dependent files checkmark Linux on x86_64 bit and click on Confirm Selection.

You will get warning as below. Go through the Note and click on Continue.

In the next screen click on Next.

Click on NON-ABAP and you find that only DE and EN are selected by default. Select any other languages you require for installation.

TIP: The files are selected above for download only. Later on during installation, you have to select the languages in the SUM tool.

In the next screen click on Download Stack XML, Download Stack Text File, Download PDF and Export to Excel to download the files to your desktop. Out of these you will have to ftp the stack.xml file to the server. It will look something like this – MP_Stack_1002146477_20241122_.xml

TIP: Before you click on Push to Download Basket on next screen, please ensure that download basket is empty so that the download contains only the files needed for SAP S/4HANA installation.

Click on Push to Download Basket. This will load the Download Basket with files you need.

In the pdf file downloaded above, in the Planned Changes section there is a link provided that takes you directly to Service Marketplace to download the media.

Download all the media and separate the following into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia/download. This includes files from Maintenance Planner 

You need the files below for the installation, please check if these are included in the maintenance planner download:

SWPM20SP19_1-80003424.SAR – Latest version of Software Provisioning Manager
SUM20SP21_5-80002456.SAR - Latest version of Software Update Manager

SAP HANA DATABASE:
HANA Database and Database Client media is already provided in the Maintenance Planner download basket.

Please refer SAP Note 3426239 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Package Stack 02: Additional Release Information for SAP HANA database release details. However, please check Note to ensure there are no problems with the SAP HANA release you select. Refer SAP Note 2655761 - SAP S/4HANA - restrictions and recommendations regarding specific revisions of SAP HANA database for use in SAP S/4HANA

IMDB_SERVER20_080_0-80002031.SAR - Latest version of SAP HANA Database as of this blog (Linux x86_64 64bit)

IMDB_CLIENT20_022_32-80002082.SAR -  SAP HANA Client (Linux x86_64 64bit)

If you want to install LiveCache you will require the SAP HANA database LCAPPS plugin from Software Downloads => SUPPORT PACKAGES AND PATCHES => SAP HANA PLATFORM EDIT 1.0 =>  HANA LCAPPS => SAP HANA LCAPPS 1.00 

TIP: If the above path is not available, please search for below file in the url https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/softwarecenter/support/index 
IMDB_LCAPPS_2080_0-20010426.SAR - Corresponding version of SAP HANA LCAPPS (Linux x86_64 64bit)

Download all the media and separate the DB files IMDB* into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia

OTHER FILES:
In addition, please download file/s for best practices content activation from SAP Note 34455517 - SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Cloud, private edition FP-Stack 02 (10/2024) content activation note. The file will be in this format BPS4CONT202302_0_0-80008741.ZIP

crlbag.p7s from https://tcs.mysap.com/crl/crlbag.p7s

COMPONENT FOR INCIDENTS:

You can open OSS messages using component BC-UPG-MP

OTHER REFERENCES:

Maintenance Planner - User Guide
Maintenance Planner Help Portal
Maintenance Planner – Frequently Asked Questions

Mahesh Sardesai
Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group

entry.title: Brief Overview Stock-Specific Unit of Measure Overview In SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

Today I am writing blog for concept of SUoM in SAP EWM

Stock-Specific Unit of Measure Overview and usage

 The stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) is a second unit of measure beside the base unit of measure, which is available in the background in the stock data and in logistic-relevant documents such as inbound and outbound deliveries and warehouse tasks. This UoM covers the data capturing during the inbound process, and the use of it in the internal and outbound processes. In the outbound process, the ERP outbound delivery can be updated with the physically picked UoM, based on the SUoM. It is deployable to the customer through a delivery note and/or invoice, so that the documents reflect the physical goods.

Activate the business function EWM SCM_EWM_FND

Effects on Master Data

If you use the stock-specific unit of measure (UoM) functionality in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the alternative UoM of your stock gets an important stock attribute, as it reflects the physical packaging of your products. If you change the UoM quantity conversion factors in the product master, it affects this attribute.

Note-If you would like to define new packaging sizes for your product, you should introduce new UoMs instead, so that existing stock is not affected.

Preferred Unit of Measure

The preferred unit of measure (UoM) is the packaging size in which you usually store a product in your warehouse. You can maintain the preferred UoM in the material master at a global level and in the product master at a warehouse level via transaction code /SCWM/MAT1. The system uses the global preferred UoM in warehouse processes unless you maintain a warehouse-specific one.

The Preferred UoM is used as a proposal in below Warehouse Processes

Inbound Process

Outbound Process

Physical Inventory Process

Note-You can also use the warehouse management monitor node Stock Not In Pref. UoM to identify stock which is not in the preferred UoM. In the standard SAP monitor structure you can find this node under Stock and Bin.

SUoM in Inbound Processes

 You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in inbound processes in a variety of ways, including inbound delivery, packing, quality inspection, deconsolidation and putaway.

SUoM in Outbound Processes

You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in outbound processes in a variety of ways, including picking and outbound delivery.

 

SUoM in Physical Inventory

 You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in physical inventory processes in a variety of ways.

SUoM in Cross Processes

You can use stock-specific unit of measure (SUoM) functionality in cross processes in a variety of ways, including in opportunistic cross-docking, in the warehouse management monitor, and in conjunction with radio frequency functions. You cannot use it in conjunction with catch weight functions.

 

Conclusion- This blog will you take through concept of SUOM. It’s has several benefits by implementing it.

Please see this  blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-members/preferred-alternative-uom-for-warehouse-operation-in-ewm/ba-p/13679453

entry.title: Warehouse Requests in SAP S/4 Hana Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Hi All 

I am writing blogs about warehouse request in sap s/4 hana embedded EWM.

What is Warehouse Requests ?

The warehouse request is a document  that enables the processing of warehouse activities for a specific product. Warehouse requests are planned documents for warehouse stock movements within and outside the warehouse. They form the basis for creation of warehouse tasks, which are assigned to warehouse workers for stock movement completion. Some of the warehouse requests that can be created in embedded EWM are outbound delivery orders, inbound delivery orders, posting change documents, and so on. In the following sections, we explain the business context in which the physical inventory process can be used in real-world scenarios and how to carry these processes out in the system.

SAP S/4HANA delivery or posting change requests become warehouse requests in embedded EWM. A warehouse request is the planning document for all internal movements in embedded EWM and is used only in embedded EWM. A warehouse request is made up of a document header and document type. The document header contains general data relevant to the entire request and the entire document; the document item contains data specific to each item in the delivery document, with the option to create follow-up documents and take follow-up actions for each item, independent of the delivery document header

The Warehouse Activities for a Product are as below

  • Picking
  • Putaway
  • Posting change
  • Stock transfer (within warehouse)
  • Scrapping

Type of warehouse requests

  • Inbound delivery
  • Outbound delivery order
  • Posting change
  • Internal stock transfer

Configuration

Service profiles are used in embedded EWM to define how documents are processed and to define the business features of the documents. These profiles are associated with the document type and item type in embedded EWM and control their features and behavior.

.There are two types of service profiles in embedded EWM.

 

System profile

System profiles are included in the scope of the standard embedded EWM system and can’t be modified. Internally, system profiles are linked to both document category profiles related to header settings and to item categories for item profile settings.

 

Customer profile

Customer profiles are required to manage individual business processes and can be modified based on business requirements. SAP has provided a set of predefined customer profiles, each of which is linked to a corresponding system profile. The customer profiles are assigned to document types and item types based on the specific business process for which they are used.

The various service profiles available in embedded EWM are explained in the following  sections

 

Process Code Profile

Process codes are used in warehouse requests to perform quantity adjustments at the item level. A process code profile determines which process codes are available for use in a warehouse request and is assigned at the item type level. SAP provides predefined process code profiles that are assigned to the corresponding system profiles. If you want to use different process codes for a business process, you can create a new process code profile and assign it to the system profile.

You can also create new process codes for a business process

IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Define Process Codes

 

 

To create a new process code profile or make changes to an existing one, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Maintain Process Code Profiles

 

 

 

Status Profile

A status profile is used to control the status of a warehouse request to allow or prohibit processing from certain business transactions. You can use status management to set a status in a document for information purposes only or to change a document status after transaction execution.

 

To define a status profile, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Codes • Define Status Profile

 

 

 

Quantity Offsetting Profile

Define the quantity offsetting profile  IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Quantity Offsetting • Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles

 

 

 

Text Profile

Use text types to copy text in the SAP S/4HANA delivery to the warehouse request in embedded EWM. You can also use text types to add text directly to the embedded EWM delivery. A text profile is used to group together text types for a delivery header or item. You can create text types for the delivery header and delivery item levels. Text types at the delivery header level include shipping instructions, bills of lading, and so on.

 

To define the text profiles for delivery documents via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Text Management • Define Text Profiles with Access Sequence

 

 

 

Field Control Profile

A field control is used to determine which fields are displayed as display-only or modifiable fields during delivery processing. A field control profile contains all allowed fields and their attributes for a document or item type.

define a field control profile via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Field Control • Define Field Control Profiles

 

 

 

 

 

 

Incompleteness Check Profile

Embedded EWM allows the system to perform an incompleteness check on a docu[1]ment to ensure that all required fields are filled before a document can be processed. These mandatory field names are mapped to a logical field name and contained in an incompleteness profile, which is assigned to a delivery document type for header fields and a delivery item type for item-related fields.

Defined IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Incompleteness Check • Define Incompleteness Profile

 

 

 

Action Profile

Embedded EWM uses the PPF for message/output control in delivery processing. All actions in a warehouse request document are generated by the framework based on action definitions and processed by the system. These actions can be print commands, email alerts, and so on. The actions are stored in an action profile assigned to a document type in EWM.

 

Set a PPF action profile via IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Actions • Change Actions and Conditions • Define Action Profiles and Actions.

 

 

 

Partner Profile

A delivery document in embedded EWM contains partner information required for further processing of the warehouse request. For example, the ship-to party is a man[1]datory business partner required in an outbound delivery order to determine the ship-to address for the delivery items. Partner processing provides the ability to maintain partner information at the delivery header or item level.

Define partner roles IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Partner Processing • Define Partner Roles

 

 

 

To create or change customer partner profiles IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Partner Processing • Define Partner Profiles

 

 

Reference Document Profile

 

A reference document profile allows you to add one or more preceding document references in embedded EWM. Each document is categorized using a reference document category code.

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Reference Document Categories

 

Once the reference document categories are defined, they can be assigned to a reference document profile

IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Reference Document Types Profiles

 

 

Date Profile

In embedded EWM, it’s possible to add new date types in warehouse request delivery documents to capture the date and time of various system processes for the document. SAP has provided predefined date types, but you can add new date/time types.

IMG-Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Date/Times • Define Date Types

 

 

The new date/time types are defined, they can be bundled into date profiles

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Reference Documents • Define Date Profile

 

 

Process Profile

Embedded EWM uses process profiles for delivery headers and items, which control the processes allowed in a delivery document. Embedded EWM supports separate process profiles for delivery headers and items.

To define a process profile for a delivery header via IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery Warehouse Requests • Process Profile • Define Process Profile for Delivery Header

 

 

 

You can create process profiles for a document item

 IMG• Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Process Profile • Define Process Profile for Delivery Item

 

To create a number range for warehouse requests, navigate to IMG • Extended Warehouse Management • Cross-Process Settings • Delivery-Warehouse Requests • Number Ranges

 

Using Inactive and Active Document Versions

EWM can accept or reject change messages for inbound deliveries from your enterprise management system, for example SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP. EWM uses inactive and active document versions for this. If warehouse activities (follow-on activities) have already begun for inbound deliveries, EWM rejects change messages from the enterprise management system. For example, EWM has already created a warehouse task (WT) for an inbound delivery. In doing so, EWM has also already set the status type Warehouse Activities to the status value Partially Completed. In the case of outbound deliveries, EWM can only accept a reduction of the quantity from the enterprise management system. If warehouse activities (follow-on activities) have already begun for inbound deliveries, EWM rejects the message from the enterprise management system.

Prerequisites

The document type of your request document contains an action profile with an action for activating the request document, for example /SCDL/IDR or /SCDL/ODR.

Note - If you do not want to activate your request documents immediately, either create your own action profile or do not assign an action profile to your request document type.

 

Please provide your feed back to improve it 

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/31d75d84edc2489b8124b0afaf3c374d.html

entry.title: Lean Warehouse Overview and Implementation Benefits
entry.content:
Hi

Today I am going to share this blog for Lean WM Implementation benefits.

Lean Warehouse Management in SAP

The lean warehouse solution focuses on simplicity and efficiency. It is tailored for businesses with less complex warehousing needs, often characterized by lower volumes and fewer product variations. Lean warehouses streamline operations by minimizing unnecessary processes and focusing on the core functionalities necessary for effective warehouse management. When you implement the Warehouse Management System (WMS), you manage goods movements and stock changes in the warehouse at storage bin level. However, if you implement Lean WM.  inventory management takes place solely at storage location level. The system does not update the stock data at storage bin level using the quants. You use Lean WM solely for processing goods receipts and goods issues. Using Lean WM, you process the warehouse movements in basically the same way as if using the Warehouse Management System: you work with deliveries, and you create transfer orders for these deliveries. These transfer orders serve as pick lists

Functionalities and Features

Lean warehouses offer a pared-down set of functionalities that cover essential warehouse operations, including:

- Basic inbound and outbound processing

- Simple inventory management

- Standard RF transactions

- Basic reporting and analytics

The use of transfer orders in Lean WM provides the following advantages:

You can reprint transfer orders at any time.

You can split transfer orders and thus distribute the workload better among the staff in your warehouse.

You can pass on the transfer order target data and the names of those who process transfer orders to the HR application component, where this data can be used to calculate incentive wage.

If you do no wish to update stocks in your fixed bin warehouse at storage bin level, Lean WM provides you with the option of using the advantages of warehouse management through transfer orders in the same way as warehouse management using the WMS. Since Lean WM does not update stocks at storage bin level, you can display the stock quantities solely in Inventory Management (IM) and not with the stock overview function in WM. Also, you cannot process possible stock differences that occur in the warehouse using Lean WM, only using Inventory Management (MM-IM)

 

 

Implementation and Maintenance

SAP recommends that you implement Lean WM if you wish to pick deliveries in a warehouse that you are not managing using WMS. Lean warehouse implementations are quicker and less resource-intensive. The streamlined nature of the solution means fewer customizations and simpler configuration processes. Maintenance is also more straightforward, with fewer components to manage and update, resulting in lower ongoing costs.

Prerequisites

You can only implement Lean WM in a fixed bin warehouse; random storage is not possible. Before you can implement Lean WM, you need to make the following configuration changes in Customizing:

  1. Assign a (newly defined) storage location to a warehouse number.
  2. Set up at least two storage types:
  • a picking storage type as the source storage type
  • A shipping area as the destination storage type for deliverie

Configuration

In order to be able to use Lean WM in your warehouse, you must first execute the following settings in Customizing for Shipping.

IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Map following activity as Highlighted descriptions are coming under IMG- Logistic Execution-Shipping- Picking- Lean WM

Defining control parameters and number ranges for the warehouse number.

Note-We recommend that you always use warehouse number 100 as a warehouse to copy when you implement Lean WM.

Defining storage type for Lean WM.

In the SAP standard version, different examples of Lean WM storage types are preset in warehouse number 100.

Defining the picking area

In the material master, a picking area must be assigned to the material in the view Plant data / Storage 1. In the SAP standard version, two picking areas are preset.

Note-Lean WM is a prerequisite for implementation of picking areas.

Defining transfer types

In the SAP standard version, transfer type A (outbound delivery) is preset for warehouse number 100 (Lean WM). 

Defining movement types

The movement type for Lean WM supplies the information that is required for inbound and outbound deliveries.

  • Destination storage type that appears on the printout of the transfer order.
  • Control indicator for processing, confirming, and issuing transfer orders (single print or combined print).
  • Movement types 101 , 601 , and 255 are preset for Lean WM. SAP recommends keeping these movement types.

 

Defining difference indicator

the difference indicator, you can classify stock differences. This indicator is used in Lean WM for evaluation purposes only.

Defining print control

 

Synchronizing movement types between Inventory Management (MM-IM) and the Warehouse Management System (Lean WM)

Note-In the SAP standard version, all the relevant movement types are preset. SAP recommends working with the standard version.

Defining degree of activation for Warehouse Management (selection between Lean WM and full WM)

Note-Control Plant / Storage Location / Warehouse Number Assignment

 

Scalability and Flexibility

 

While lean warehouses provide sufficient flexibility for many small to medium-sized businesses, they might face limitations when scaling up to meet the demands of a rapidly growing enterprise or more complex logistical requirements

Cost Considerations

Lean warehouse solutions are more cost-effective, with lower upfront costs and reduced long-term financial commitments. This makes them an attractive option for businesses operating on tighter budgets or those seeking a quicker return on investment

Conclusion

lean warehouse solutions depend largely on the specific needs and goals of your business. if simplicity, cost-effectiveness, and quicker implementation are your priorities, a lean warehouse approach may be more suitable.

 

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/ceadbd53d34ab64ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg focused on using AI to make apps easier to build and use
entry.content:
SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg was a great success. It was our third and final event in 2024, held on November 19 in our AppHaus in Heidelberg. The goal was to empower developers, architects, and technical consultants with knowledge on how to build SAP Fiori apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and all the new AI functionality that we are building into the SAP Fiori elements framework.

 

We started by asking the attendees to define SAP Fiori. Here’s what they came up with:

 

Our customers seem to be aligned with us on what SAP Fiori means.

[ALT TEXT: A word cloud created by the attendees with the prompt “what is SAP Fiori?” The biggest words in the cloud are innovation, UX, user experience, design, and modern.]

 

It was very gratifying to see the results, as the participants have the same view of SAP Fiori that SAP does – a design system for creating modern and innovative user experiences.

 

We then launched into the day, showing examples of the SAP Fiori user experience across SAP S/4HANA, including many of the innovations that we have recently added to our public cloud version. The day proceeded as follows:

 

[ALT TEXT: graphic of the schedule for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg]

 

SAP Fiori is the face of SAP S/4HANA Cloud

The starting point for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is My Home. It provides an easy way to find and launch all your SAP Fiori apps. Most of those apps will be based on SAP Fiori elements. My Home includes a new “recommended” tab that uses AI to recommend apps to a user based on their role in the system. This makes it faster and easier for someone to get started with SAP S/4HANA.

 

[ALT TEXT: screen shot of My Home for SAP S/4HANA with the different tabs for apps displayed.]

 

From My Home, people launch the apps they need to complete their work. We use SAP Fiori elements to build around 80% of new apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It saves developers time on creating the user interface, drives consistency across apps, and ensures that all the enterprise functionality that people expect (accessibility, internationalization, responsiveness, security, etc.) are built into the applications.

 

[ALT TEXT: Two SAP Fiori elements apps in SAP S/4HANA, one showing a list report and the other an object page.]

 

AI powers application generation

Since the event was focused on developers, we spent lots of time showing our latest AI-driven tools for creating applications. In SAP Fiori tools 2403, we introduced the ability to generate applications from a business requirements document. At innovation day, we showed a lab preview of an application generator that uses an image of the app as the starting point. This was the foundation of the hands-on session where participants could sketch an image on paper or the white board and generate an SAP Fiori elements application from that. The AI creates the CAP service, the application, and even sample data so you can test your app. We plan to release this soon. The hands-on allowed participants to try SAP Fiori elements, the flexible programming model, SAP Fiori tools, and the planned AI capabilities in the context of creating a contract management application.

 

SAP Fiori is incorporating more AI for business users

We ended the day with some previews of planned AI functionality that will first be released into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editon.

  • Easy filter – natural language searching in SAP Fiori elements list reports
  • Smart summarization – AI-created summary of SAP Fiori elements object pages
  • Assisted error resolution – providing more context on system errors with recommendations to resolve them
  • Recap – a summary of what you missed when you were away from your SAP system
  • Joule-assisted insight card creation in My Home– ask Joule for the information you need and create a card that you can add to My Home

Some of these are currently in beta testing with selected public cloud customers.

 

It takes great teamwork to put on SAP Fiori Innovation Day

Putting on an event like this is a major undertaking with many contributors. Thank you to all the SAP presenters (in order of appearance): Katja Zoch, Sebastian Steinhauer, Thomas Reiss, Ioannis Grammatikakis, Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Thorsten Lange, Ian Quigley; hands-on facilitators: Katrin Polloczek and Christoph Gollmick; partner presenters (also in order of appearance): Michael Englbrecht from eXXcellent solutions, Christophe Chan Hin from sovanta with Sören Schulz from Die Continetale, and Dr. Felix Aller & Christoph Ueberle from cbs Corporate Business Solutions; and panelists: Tina Butz from cbs, Christian Heinrich from sovanta, Michael Englbrecht from eXXcellent solutions, Ioannis Grammatikakis, head of UX Engineering at SAP.

 

There were also many SAP colleagues who worked behind the scenes to create all the demos and hands-on exercises. And, of course, the AppHaus team, led by Beate Riefer, who handled all the on-site logistics. Finally, a huge thanks to Sebastian Werner, who worked closely with me to bring this event to life.

 

Most of all, thank you to all the customers that attended, asked insightful questions, and engaged with us all and all the partners during the breaks and after hours.  

 

 

 

Next steps

  • Join us at an SAP Fiori Development Innovation Day in 2025 – vote on the locations where we should host the events
  • Register for the monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable call to meet other developers and architects who build SAP Fiori apps.
  • Subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter, in which we share updates on SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, SAPUI5, and SAP Build Code every other month.  
  • Read about the other SAP Fiori Innovation Days we hosted in Dublin and Chicago.

entry.title: Unleashing ABAP Potential: An introduction to Extension Libraries (XCO)
entry.content:
Introduction

In the evolving world of SAP development, the Extension Libraries (XCO) in ABAP stand as a significant innovation. Designed for consistency, simplicity, and future readiness, XCO enables developers to extend their ABAP capabilities effortlessly. Moreover, XCO ensures compatibility across diverse ABAP environments, including traditional On-Premise systems and the ABAP environment on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).
